faqs.question: How much does squirrel removal cost in Farmingdale?
faqs.answer: Squirrel removal in Farmingdale typically ranges from $900 to $2,500, depending on the number of squirrels, entry points, and extent of damage. This includes humane trapping, exclusion work to seal entry points, and cleanup. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove squirrels from my Farmingdale home?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day emergency service throughout Farmingdale and can typically begin trapping within 24 hours of your call. Most squirrel removal projects are completed within 3-7 days, though larger infestations may take up to two weeks to ensure all animals are safely removed and all entry points are sealed.

faqs.question: What damage do squirrels cause in Farmingdale attics?
faqs.answer: Squirrels in Farmingdale homes commonly chew through electrical wiring (creating fire hazards), damage insulation, tear up roof shingles and soffits, and leave droppings that can harbor diseases. They're particularly active in fall and spring when seeking nesting sites. Our service includes damage assessment and repair recommendations.

faqs.question: Do you offer warranty on squirrel exclusion work in Farmingdale?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide a comprehensive warranty on all exclusion work performed in Farmingdale. If squirrels re-enter through any area we've sealed, we'll return to fix the problem at no additional charge. Our exclusion methods are designed to permanently prevent squirrel entry while maintaining your home's aesthetic appeal.
